evidence.text: LTL tonnage per day decreased 4.8% in the third quarter of 2024 due primarily to a decrease in LTL shipments per day and a decrease in LTL weight per shipment.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Evidence shows a clear decline in demand with a 4.8% decrease in LTL tonnage per day.

factors.summary: The company is successfully raising prices.

evidence.text: Our LTL revenue per hundredweight increased 1.5% and 3.3% in the third quarter and first nine months of 2024, respectively, as compared to the same periods of 2023. Excluding fuel surcharges, LTL revenue per hundredweight increased 4.6% and 5.3% in the third quarter and first nine months of 2024, respectively, as compared to the same periods in 2023 due primarily to the success of our long-term yield management strategy.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Company successfully increased LTL revenue per hundredweight by 1.5% to 5.3%, indicating strong pricing power.

evidence.text: Revenue decreased $45.1 million, or 3.0%, in the third quarter of 2024 as compared to the same period of 2023 due to a decrease in volumes that was partially offset by an increase in LTL revenue per hundredweight.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Revenue growth decelerated by 3.0% due to decreased volumes, indicating a moderate headwind.

evidence.text: LTL tonnage per day decreased 4.8% in the third quarter of 2024 due primarily to a decrease in LTL shipments per day and a decrease in LTL weight per shipment.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Unit volumes are declining, evidenced by a 4.8% decrease in LTL tonnage per day.

evidence.text: The cost of diesel fuel, excluding fuel taxes, represents the largest component of operating supplies and expenses, and can vary based on both average price per gallon and consumption. Our average cost per gallon of diesel fuel decreased 20.5% and 12.4% in the third quarter and first nine months of 2024, respectively, as compared to the same periods last year.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Input costs declined significantly, with diesel fuel costs decreasing by 20.5% in the third quarter.

evidence.text: Salaries, wages and benefits increased $17.4 million, or 2.6%, in the third quarter of 2024 as compared to the third quarter of 2023, due to a $8.3 million increase in salaries and wages and a $9.1 million increase in employee benefit costs.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Labor costs increased by 2.6%, reflecting a moderate headwind in expense management.

evidence.text: Our operating ratio increased to 72.7% in the third quarter of 2024 from 70.6% in the same period of 2023, indicating a decline in profitability.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Margins are compressing, as evidenced by a 2.1% increase in the operating ratio, indicating declining profitability.

evidence.text: Our P&D and platform shipments per hour both improved in the third quarter of 2024 as compared to the third quarter of 2023, which helped offset the reduction in our linehaul laden load factor.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Operational efficiency improved, with P&D and platform shipments per hour increasing, indicating positive developments.
